samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection module for detecting rising edges in data_in.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_START">State indicating the start of a pulse.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_END">State indicating the end of a pulse.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <wire id="next_state">Next state for the state machine.</wire>
        <always @(*) begin
            case(state)
                IDLE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1 && ~data_in_prev) begin
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = IDLE;
                    end
                end
                PULSE_START: begin
                    if(~data_in) begin
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                    end
                end
                PULSE_END: begin
                    if(data_in) begin
                        next_state = IDLE;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    end
                end
                default: next_state = IDLE;
            endcase
        end
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state <= IDLE;
            end else begin
                state <= next_state;
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        Always block triggered on the positive edge of the clk signal and the negative edge of the rst_n signal.
        
        In the reset condition (~rst_n), initialize the state register to the IDLE state and set the data_out output to 0, indicating no pulse detected.
        
        For each state, determine the next state based on the current state and the values of data_in and data_in_prev.
        
        Update the state register with the next state during the positive edge of the clk signal.
        
        Set data_out to 1 if the current state is PULSE_END, indicating the end of a pulse, otherwise set it to 0.
    </implementation>
    
    <task id="reset_task">
        <description>Task to reset the state register and data_out output.</description>
        <step>Set state register to IDLE state.</step>
        <step>Set data_out output to 0.</step>
    </task>
    
    <task id="update_state_task">
        <description>Task to update the state register based on the current state and inputs.</description>
        <step>Check the current state and inputs to determine the next state.</step>
        <step>Update the state register with the next state.</step>
    </task>
    
    <task id="set_data_out_task">
        <description>Task to set the data_out output based on the current state.</description>
        <step>Check the current state to determine the value of data_out.</step>
        <step>Set data_out output accordingly.</step>
    </task>
    
    <task id="main_task">
        <description>Main task to control the operation of the pulse detect module.</description>
        <step>Trigger the reset_task when rst_n is low.</step>
        <step>Trigger the update_state_task on the positive edge of clk.</step>
        <step>Trigger the set_data_out_task on the positive edge of clk.</step>
    </main_task>
endmodule
